title: 'Alien: Romulus' Wins Busy Box Office Weekend With $41.5M Opening, $108M Globally
text: Director Fede Álvarez's sci-fi horror film dominated the domestic box office with a $45.1 million opening weekend, exceeding expectations that had projected the high-$20 million range. The film achieved a worldwide total of $108.2 million during its debut, with $66.7 million generated internationally. The eighth installment in the Alien franchise performed particularly well by attracting both younger and older male audiences. The movie earned "a near-franchise best B+ CinemaScore" while maintaining an 82% critics score on Rotten Tomatoes. Set between the original 1979 film and 1986's Aliens, the R-rated film follows young colonists who encounter a derelict space station and the alien creature. It stars Cailee Spaeny, David Jonsson, Archie Renaux, Isabela Merced, and Spike Fearn. The film displaced Marvel's "Deadpool & Wolverine" from the top position after three consecutive weeks, though the superhero film continued performing strongly in second place with $29 million, bringing its domestic total to $545.9 million. Sony's "It Ends With Us" placed third, while the "Coraline" rerelease notably earned $8.9 million, setting a record for Fathom Classics releases.
